source_materials.label: ePrint 2013/676
source_materials.notes: Provides the paper-facing validation anchor for the 5-round checkpoint.

source_materials.label: PRESENT CHES 2007
source_materials.notes: Defines PRESENT round structure and key schedule.

primitive_overview.state_layout_summary: The encryption state is a 64-bit state updated round by round. The current lane treats the state in bit-oriented form and also groups bits into 16 parallel 4-bit S-box cells where needed.
primitive_overview.round_structure_summary: Each full round applies addRoundKey, then the 16-way 4-bit S-box layer, then the bit permutation layer pLayer.
primitive_overview.key_schedule_summary: The 80-bit master-key register is rotated left by 61 positions each round, the top 4 bits pass through the PRESENT S-box, and the round counter is xor-ed into designated key-register bits before the next round key is extracted.
primitive_overview.notes: This overview is paper-facing and reviewable. It is not yet the final IR graph.

analysis_task.security_goal: paper-checkpoint validation for a distinguisher-support metric used in later attack construction
analysis_task.task_summary: Check whether the 5-round PRESENT-80 related-key differential lane reaches the same minimum active S-box count reported in the paper.

validation_anchor.paper_title: Automatic Security Evaluation and (Related-key) Differential Characteristic Search: Application to SIMON, PRESENT, LBlock, DES(L) and Other Bit-oriented Block Ciphers
validation_anchor.claim: Table 4 reports that the minimum number of active S-boxes for 5-round PRESENT-80 is 3.
validation_anchor.comparison_metric: exact minimum active S-box count at round end 5
validation_anchor.notes: This is the checkpoint later used by the verdict layer.

state_containers.name: round_counter
state_containers.role: per-round counter injected into the key schedule

schedule_indexing_model.movement_rule: The same 80-bit key register persists across rounds. Each round exposes bits 79..16 as the round key, then mutates the same register in place by rotate, top-nibble S-box application, and round-counter injection.
schedule_indexing_model.summary: PRESENT-80 uses a single fixed key register rather than a rolling queue of key words.

round_steps.name: addRoundKey
round_steps.summary: Xor the 64-bit state with the current 64-bit round key.

round_steps.name: S-box layer
round_steps.summary: Apply the 4-bit PRESENT S-box in parallel to 16 state nibbles.

semantic_refinement_requirements.summary: Later stages need the PRESENT 4-bit S-box differential/activity semantics instead of treating this step as an opaque nonlinear box.

round_steps.name: pLayer
round_steps.summary: Permute the 64 state bits according to the fixed PRESENT bit-permutation table.

key_schedule_steps.name: Extract round key
key_schedule_steps.summary: Take the left-most 64 bits of the 80-bit key register as the round key.

indexing_effect.summary: Expose a fixed top slice of the same 80-bit register as the current round key without changing schedule indexing.

key_schedule_steps.name: Rotate key register
key_schedule_steps.summary: Rotate the 80-bit key register left by 61 positions.

indexing_effect.summary: Mutate the same 80-bit register in place; there is no queue head or slot advance.

key_schedule_steps.name: S-box on top nibble
key_schedule_steps.summary: Apply the PRESENT S-box to the top 4 bits of the key register.

semantic_refinement_requirements.required_for: related-key propagation through the key-schedule nonlinear step
semantic_refinement_requirements.summary: The key-schedule S-box needs the same PRESENT differential/activity rule pack instead of being left as free text.

indexing_effect.summary: Only the top nibble of the same fixed register is rewritten; schedule indexing still does not move.

key_schedule_steps.name: Inject round counter
key_schedule_steps.summary: Xor the round counter into the designated key-register bit positions.

indexing_effect.summary: Inject the counter into fixed bit positions of the same register; no queue advance or slot selection is involved.

explicit_facts: PRESENT-80 is an SPN block cipher with 64-bit block size and 80-bit key size.
The cipher uses 31 rounds in the standard encryption algorithm.
Each round applies addRoundKey, S-box layer, and pLayer.
The attack paper reports that the minimum number of active S-boxes for 5-round PRESENT-80 is 3.
The current lane targets a related-key differential xor-difference setting.
inferred_facts: For the current automated lane, the relevant security goal is best represented as validation of a paper-facing checkpoint rather than full end-to-end attack reconstruction.
The analysis target is treated as bit-granular because the current benchmark lane is bit-oriented and the reference case is modeled that way.
unresolved_questions: How much of the later key-recovery stage from the paper should be represented already at the algorithm-description layer?
Should future algorithm descriptions include explicit distinction between primitive description and attack-program description when a paper mixes both?
How should we encode paper-specific modeling assumptions that are not part of the primitive itself?
blocking_gaps: The current schema now exposes the needed constants and hooks, but the operator-parameter vocabulary is still permissive rather than canonical across primitive families.
Semantic refinement requirements can now be declared, but they are not yet auto-resolved to concrete local rule packs during compilation.
The current top-level report layer is still closer to a workflow report than a full cryptanalysis narrative.
provenance_and_gaps.notes: This example is intentionally richer than the intake card but still earlier than the primitive spec plus backend instantiation split.
